scope: Normalize candidate AI workers, agent-company control-plane assets, research infrastructure, and money-making tools into a local-only evaluation queue.

source_policy.notes: This refresh used existing local artifacts under E:\agent-company-lab. It did not query GitHub, browse external pages, call APIs, install dependencies, start runtimes, or execute untrusted candidate code.

source_artifacts.role: candidate-registry-curator active goal and peer worker handoff map
capability classes, overlap boundaries, and required gates
source observations, proof artifacts, and stop gates for money-making routes

normalization_rules: Each candidate must point to local source evidence already present in the lab.
Each candidate must name the first local proof artifact or fixture before any adoption.
External repos are reference, adapter, or future-runtime candidates only; none are adopted.
Money-making routes can create local drafts, parser fixtures, approval packets, or paper-only worksheets only.
Existing agent-company assets are promoted only after overlap review and local evaluation evidence.
Every candidate keeps account, browser, dependency, runtime, model/API/MCP, public-action, wallet/payment, security-testing, trade, and submission gates explicit.

candidates.why_test: Keeps the CEO context-light while preserving state, decisions, tasks, and gates for continuity.
candidates.first_local_proof: Diff the packet against current roster, manager packets, and latest trail artifacts; prove it omits raw customer material and preserves action gates.
candidates.overlap_review: Confirm it complements, not duplicates, continuity-watchdog restore packets.

candidates.why_test: Separates true human-required gates from optional approvals so workers do not stall or invent authority.
candidates.first_local_proof: Run a local classification review against current service-request, account, payment, public-action, and runtime gates.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with browser-account-ops-worker and premium-customer-context-router boundaries.

candidates.why_test: Closes the ownerless-work gap without restarting workers or assigning service requests.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a local-only acknowledgement state reducer and stale-owner fixture; prove zero dispatch side effects.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with lane-manager-ai_resources_lab and continuity-watchdog-worker.

candidates.why_test: Detects stale, offline, ownerless, overlapping, and goal-less work before worker restarts.
candidates.first_local_proof: Generate a local stale-lane fixture from existing reports and emit restore/escalation packets only.
candidates.overlap_review: Do not duplicate ai_resources owner acknowledgement checks.

candidates.why_test: Separates AI-doable browser/account preparation from human KYC, billing, tax, legal, and terms gates.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a local packet template that marks each action as AI-preparable, human-gated, or blocked.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with human_action_feed_v1 and service-worker gate maps.

candidates.why_test: Existing local adapter can evaluate prompt and worker shapes without paid model calls.
candidates.first_local_proof: Run fixture-only evaluation against saved request summaries and compare outputs to prompt safety expectations.
candidates.overlap_review: Keep it as a harness, not a model-backed worker.

candidates.why_test: Preserves future tool, handoff, and guardrail shapes without running model-backed agents.
candidates.first_local_proof: Validate a manifest-only packet that maps agent, tool, handoff, model, and cost fields to local gates.
candidates.overlap_review: Do not register as worker until model/provider/cost/secrets gates exist.

candidates.why_test: High-fit browser-agent runtime candidate for future read-only browser-worker patterns.
candidates.first_local_proof: Write a no-install adapter contract comparison against browser_worker_adapter_contract_v1 and signed approval guards.
candidates.overlap_review: Compare against existing browser plugin/control surface before any new browser worker hire.

candidates.why_test: Best-fit deterministic foundation for approved future browser workers and visual verification.
candidates.first_local_proof: Compare current in-app browser control patterns with Playwright policy gates using docs already in the lab.
candidates.overlap_review: Prefer existing in-app browser tooling until an exact worker scope needs Playwright.

candidates.why_test: Potential future MCP route for browser inspection if registry, prompt-injection, and browser-start gates mature.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a threat-model checklist only; no MCP server start or browser session.
candidates.overlap_review: Must not bypass MCP tool registry or browser-account-ops gates.

candidates.why_test: Useful architecture evidence for checkpoints, interrupts, human review, and agent inboxes.
candidates.first_local_proof: Extract a local checkpoint-interrupt fixture shape; do not install LangGraph.
candidates.overlap_review: Map to existing checkpoint_interrupt_contract_v1 before any framework adoption.

candidates.why_test: Reference for long-running service-worker workflows and durable history replay.
candidates.first_local_proof: Draft a service-request history replay manifest using only existing SQLite/report data.
candidates.overlap_review: Compare with DBOS, Restate, Hatchet, and current SQLite control plane.

candidates.why_test: Reference for journaled tool and LLM step recovery.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a report-only journaled-step reducer fixture against copied local rows.
candidates.overlap_review: Compare with Temporal and existing egress ledger before runtime adoption.

candidates.why_test: Closest external pattern to a database-first durable workflow posture.
candidates.first_local_proof: Write a transactional workflow reducer fixture using saved service-request rows only.
candidates.overlap_review: Only revisit if SQLite control-plane limits are proven by local evidence.

candidates.why_test: Useful reference for MCP/A2A registry governance and tool discovery controls.
candidates.first_local_proof: Map its registry concepts to existing mcp_tool_registry_gate_v1 and unified egress gateway docket.
candidates.overlap_review: Reference only until local MCP registry gates require a concrete implementation.

candidates.why_test: Reference for unified policy, RBAC, spend, and observability around model/tool traffic.
candidates.first_local_proof: Compare concepts to the local unified_agent_egress_gateway_docket_v1 without starting a gateway.
candidates.overlap_review: Must preserve local egress ledger authority.

candidates.why_test: Turns traces, evals, costs, and replay into CEO operating memory without telemetry export.
candidates.first_local_proof: Design a local trace replay packet from existing outcomes/artifacts only.
candidates.overlap_review: Use existing trace/outcome tables before adding telemetry tooling.

candidates.why_test: Useful role taxonomy and groupchat evidence for multi-agent staffing, not runtime adoption.
candidates.first_local_proof: Extract role/team labels into a capability-overlap map and compare to current roster.
candidates.overlap_review: Existing owners must evolve before new hires.

candidates.why_test: Human approval UX may help route exact operator decisions, but notification/public-action surfaces are risky.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a local approval-state taxonomy and reject any notification or public-action integration.
candidates.overlap_review: Manual architecture review required before any deeper work.

candidates.why_test: Already normalizes 27 external repos into reference, adapter, future-runtime, or blocked-dependency dispositions.
candidates.first_local_proof: Use it as an input fixture for candidate registry validation and stale-source detection.
candidates.overlap_review: Owned by AI Resources; avoid re-fetching sources unless scoped.

candidates.why_test: Prevents unsafe generic worker pools by splitting browser, durable runtime, gateway, model-backed, observability, and workflow classes.
candidates.first_local_proof: Feed candidate rows through capability classes and flag duplicate/overlap candidates.
candidates.overlap_review: This is the overlap gate for future hires.

candidates.why_test: Ranks framework adapter candidates while forcing zero runtime adoptions.
candidates.first_local_proof: Update the scorecard input mapping with this registry and confirm selected_for_runtime_adoption_count remains zero.
candidates.overlap_review: Use as a score input only, not an adoption decision.

candidates.why_test: Reusable local harness for checking whether worker prompts preserve stop gates.
candidates.first_local_proof: Add candidate-registry stop-gate cases and run only local fixture evals.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with local-evaluation-harness-builder.

candidates.why_test: Can shape future typed worker proposals while preserving paper-only/runtime-blocked boundaries.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a typed proposal for one registry candidate without registering or starting a worker.
candidates.overlap_review: Must stay proposal-only until worker pool gates are signed.

candidates.why_test: Highest-priority local money route with no-contact offer-matrix proof.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a no-send Upwork approval request packet or offer matrix from saved source observations.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with lead_generation_and_sales manager; do not create a duplicate worker.

candidates.why_test: Near-term marketplace route with existing local approval packets and no listing/public-action authority.
candidates.first_local_proof: Build a local approval rubric for the six PromptBase approval packets.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with digital_products_templates_plugins manager.

candidates.why_test: Paid-code route can be hardened with duplicate and claim-readiness checks before any public claim.
candidates.first_local_proof: Refresh only the local claim-readiness and duplicate-check packet from saved evidence.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with paid_code_bounties manager.

candidates.why_test: Prize upside exists; local toy harness can test reproducibility discipline without official data or submission.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create ARC micro-environment replay baseline using local fixtures only.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with ai_ml_competitions manager.

candidates.why_test: Supports paper-only market research from saved/public data shapes while blocking trades and accounts.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create a saved-row parser/checker that emits paper-only signal rows and kill reasons.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with prediction_market_research manager.

candidates.why_test: Turns existing agent-company assets into local product packaging and approval packets.
candidates.first_local_proof: Draft no-publish route decision packet with license, refund, support, checksum, payout, and tax gates.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with digital_products_templates_plugins manager.

candidates.why_test: Can normalize paid OSS cards into local queue rows with hard rejection for captcha/abuse rows.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create deterministic parser against saved/public card snapshots only.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with paid_code_bounties manager and source-spec registry.

candidates.why_test: Public rules parsing can prepare static/source-review candidates while blocking target testing and reports.
candidates.first_local_proof: Create no-login public-program fixture with scope, safe harbor, reward, known-issue, and kill-reason fields.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with security_bounty_private_reports manager.

candidates.why_test: Can test whether agent-company public-good tooling has a grant route without submission or wallet actions.
candidates.first_local_proof: Write a no-submit grant-fit memo with rules, wallet, KYC, project evidence, duplicate, and budget gates.
candidates.overlap_review: Coordinate with web3_airdrops_grants_hackathons manager.

recommended_next_action: Hand the 12 queued candidates to local-evaluation-harness-builder-20260621 as report-only evaluation packets. Start with ceo_state_packet_v1, human_action_feed_v1, ai_resources_owner_acknowledgement_loop, pydantic_ai_testmodel_adapter, and worker_capability_class_registry_v1 because they strengthen local control without external dependencies.
